page: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical AMD EPYC 9V45 cores with a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled, featuring 5669 GB of local disk capacity alongside Premium Storage capability. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96ads_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng fall into the top 10% of tested servers, supported by advanced vector extensions including AVX-512 and AVX_VNNI. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads reaching 9486.57 GB/sec and large-block uncached writes reaching 174.65 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations both rank in the top 10%, with Redis achieving 140.26 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference speeds for text generation remain in the top 10% across small, medium, and large models, though prompt processing for large 70B models drops to the middle 50%.

Given its high resource density, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial local storage and high-throughput compute without the premium cost of dedicated GPU hardware. The combination of 96 vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and over 5.6 TB of local storage makes this server highly suitable for relational databases, high-performance web serving, large-scale data compression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores), 384.0 GB of RAM, and a substantial 5669 GB of bundled local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier performance across multiple benchmarks, including stress-ng CPU tests, database operations, and Redis SET operations, which reaches 140.26 million operations per second. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and SHA_NI. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-capacity local storage, the D96ads_v7 is highly efficient for demanding workloads such as relational databases, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier database and CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-core AMD EPYC virtual machine featuring massive local storage and top-tier database performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs operating at 4.5 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory and 5669 GB of bundled local storage
Top-tier CPU, database, and Redis benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptography hardware acceleration
Well-suited for relational databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
